feed.title: Fred's corner
feed.subtitle: An elitist [Eclipse/VS Code] plugin author's blog about Java, Eclipse, Maven, VS Code extensions and Language Servers.

entry.title: Use GitHub Releases to host an Eclipse P2 Repository

link.title: Use GitHub Releases to host an Eclipse P2 Repository

entry.summary: Hosting an Eclipse P2 repository is a great way to share your awesome Eclipse plugins with the rest of the world. However, finding a host can be a challenge. Who wants to pay for hosting nowadays? For some time, people (including me) have (ab)used Github pages as a hosting solution. Lorenzo Bettini has a pretty thorough blog post about this. Even though Git is not meant to host binaries, it’ll ...

entry.title: Automatically detect managed JDKs in Eclipse

link.title: Automatically detect managed JDKs in Eclipse

entry.summary: I’ve been using .sdkman to easily install JDKs for a while now, and one thing that bothers me about Eclipse is that it doesn’t automatically detect the JDKs that .sdkman manages. JRE Discovery So I wrote the JRE Discovery plugin for Eclipse. It’s a simple plugin that automatically detects the JREs that Java managers install, and configures them in Eclipse. It currently detects JREs managed by ...
